q-and-a: Interview or Q&A format with an artist, band, or industry figure. Includes podcast excerpts framed as Q&A.
album-reviews: Critical review of a specific album where the writer evaluates the music and gives an opinion or rating. Not a press release or release announcement.
music-videos: Primary focus is a music video: premiere, debut, visual analysis, or reaction. Wins over single/album release categories when a video is the main subject.
tours: Tour-related coverage: tour announcements, dates, on-the-road news, festival appearances, or tour reviews.
upcoming-releases: A release not yet available at publish time. Signals: pre-orders open, release date after PUBLISHED_AT, "out on [future date]". Do NOT use when a single is already out on DSPs — use new-songs instead even if a full album is still upcoming. Use when the headline is pre-order or future album/EP date, or a teaser track ahead of a future album (not a standalone single launch).
notable-releases: A newly released album, EP, or single by a mainstream or widely-known artist (charting acts, major labels, household names). Examples: Macklemore, Tegan and Sara, Taylor Swift, Drake. Takes precedence over new-releases and new-songs when those artists appear in the title with "out now" or "available today". Genre-internal popularity alone does not qualify.
new-releases: A just-released album or EP from a non-mainstream artist when the article is a heads-up or announcement, not a critical review. Signals: "out now", "available today", full-length or EP release. Use for a digital EP already out as a preview when the EP release is the headline (even if a full album is still upcoming).
new-songs: A just-released single or track from a non-mainstream artist when the article is a heads-up, not a deep analysis. Signals: "new single", "single out now", "available on all DSPs", one song focus. Use even when a full album is announced for a later date — the single being live now wins over upcoming-releases.
songs: Deep dive on a song that is not a fresh release: throwbacks, lyrical analysis, retrospectives, or historical context. Not for release announcements or press releases.
news: Catch-all for music news that does not fit above: signings, lineup changes, label moves, lawsuits, awards, controversies, and general industry updates.
